title: Choosing a Restaurant
summary: A man and woman discuss where to eat dinner, comparing two restaurants before deciding on one that suits them both.

body.speaker: Man
body.text: Are you hungry? I thought we could go out for dinner tonight.

body.speaker: Woman
body.text: That sounds lovely! Do you have somewhere in mind?

body.speaker: Man
body.text: There's a new Thai restaurant on George Street that everyone is talking about. But there's also that Italian place we used to go to.

body.speaker: Woman
body.text: I love both, but I haven't tried Thai food in a long time. What's the Thai restaurant like?

body.speaker: Man
body.text: A colleague went last Friday and said the food was amazing. The portions are generous and it's not too expensive.

body.speaker: Woman
body.text: That sounds great. Is it easy to get a table, or do we need a reservation?

body.speaker: Man
body.text: My colleague said they accept walk-ins on weeknights, but it can get busy. I could call ahead just to be safe.

body.speaker: Woman
body.text: Yes, please do. I'd hate to arrive and find it full. What time were you thinking?

body.speaker: Man
body.text: How about seven o'clock? That gives us plenty of time to get ready.

body.speaker: Woman
body.text: Perfect. Should I wear something smart, or is it casual?

body.speaker: Man
body.text: It's a casual place, so you don't need to dress up. Just comfortable clothes.

body.speaker: Woman
body.text: Great, I'll wear my blue dress then. It's comfortable but still looks nice.

body.speaker: Man
body.text: Good idea. I'll make the booking now. Thai food tonight — I'm already looking forward to it!

body.speaker: Woman
body.text: Me too! And if we like it, we should make it our regular spot.

annotations.meaningVi: có nơi nào đó trong tâm trí, đang nghĩ đến một nơi cụ thể
annotations.exampleEn: Do you have a destination in mind for your holiday?
We used to go to that café every Sunday.
The hotel was nice and not too expensive.

annotations.meaningVi: chấp nhận khách không đặt trước
annotations.exampleEn: The salon accepts walk-ins on quiet afternoons.
I'll call ahead to check if they have a table for four.
Bring an umbrella just to be safe — it might rain.

annotations.meaningVi: nhiều thời gian, đủ thời gian
annotations.exampleEn: We have plenty of time before the train leaves.

annotations.meaningVi: ăn mặc lịch sự, diện đồ đẹp
annotations.exampleEn: You don't need to dress up — it's a casual party.
I'll make the booking online for Friday evening.

annotations.meaningVi: địa điểm thường xuyên, nơi hay lui tới
annotations.exampleEn: This café has become our regular spot on Saturday mornings.

grammarNotes.title: Used to + infinitive for past habits
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Cấu trúc 'used to + động từ nguyên mẫu' diễn tả thói quen hoặc trạng thái trong quá khứ không còn đúng nữa. Ví dụ: 'that Italian place we used to go to' có nghĩa là trước đây họ thường xuyên đến đó nhưng bây giờ không còn nữa. Lưu ý: 'used to' không được dùng cho hành động hiện tại.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: Use 'used to + infinitive' to describe past habits or states that no longer exist. Example: 'that Italian place we used to go to' means they regularly went there in the past but no longer do. It is only used for the past, never for present habits.

grammarNotes.title: Could + infinitive for possibility or polite suggestion
grammarNotes.bodyVi: 'Could' được dùng để diễn tả khả năng ('it can get busy') hoặc đề xuất lịch sự ('I could call ahead'). Khi dùng để đề nghị, 'could' nhẹ nhàng hơn 'will' hoặc 'should', cho thấy người nói đang đề xuất chứ không ra lệnh.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: 'Could' expresses possibility ('it can get busy') or a polite offer/suggestion ('I could call ahead'). As a suggestion, 'could' is softer than 'will' or 'should', showing the speaker is offering an option rather than stating a firm plan.

translationVi:
Người đàn ông: Cậu đói chưa? Tớ nghĩ tối nay chúng mình có thể ra ngoài ăn tối.
Người phụ nữ: Nghe hay đấy! Cậu có nơi nào trong tâm trí chưa?
Người đàn ông: Có một nhà hàng Thái mới trên đường George mà ai cũng đang nói đến. Nhưng cũng có nhà hàng Ý mà chúng mình hay đến trước đây.
Người phụ nữ: Tớ thích cả hai, nhưng tớ chưa thử đồ ăn Thái từ rất lâu rồi. Nhà hàng Thái đó như thế nào?
Người đàn ông: Một đồng nghiệp đã đến đó vào thứ Sáu tuần trước và nói đồ ăn ở đó tuyệt vời. Khẩu phần rất nhiều và không quá đắt.
Người phụ nữ: Nghe hay lắm. Có dễ xếp bàn không, hay chúng mình cần đặt trước?
Người đàn ông: Đồng nghiệp tớ nói họ chấp nhận khách không đặt trước vào các tối trong tuần, nhưng có thể đông. Tớ có thể gọi điện trước cho chắc.
Người phụ nữ: Vâng, xin cậu làm vậy đi. Tớ không muốn đến rồi thấy đầy bàn. Cậu nghĩ mấy giờ?
Người đàn ông: Bảy giờ nhé? Chúng mình có nhiều thời gian để chuẩn bị.
Người phụ nữ: Tuyệt. Tớ nên mặc đồ lịch sự hay thoải mái thôi?
Người đàn ông: Đó là nơi thoải mái, nên cậu không cần ăn mặc diện đâu. Chỉ cần quần áo thoải mái thôi.
Người phụ nữ: Tốt, vậy tớ sẽ mặc váy xanh. Thoải mái mà vẫn đẹp.
Người đàn ông: Ý hay đấy. Tớ đặt bàn ngay bây giờ. Tối nay ăn Thái — tớ đã trông đợi rồi!
Người phụ nữ: Tớ cũng vậy! Và nếu chúng mình thích, chúng mình nên biến đây thành điểm lui tới quen thuộc.

questions.prompt: What does the man suggest doing tonight?
questions.explanation: The man says 'I thought we could go out for dinner tonight.'
questions.hint: He wants to go somewhere to eat.

questions.prompt: Where is the new Thai restaurant?
questions.explanation: The man says 'There's a new Thai restaurant on George Street.'
questions.hint: A specific street name is mentioned.

questions.prompt: When did the man's colleague visit the Thai restaurant?
questions.explanation: The man says 'A colleague went last Friday.'
questions.hint: It was a specific day last week.

questions.prompt: What did the colleague say about the restaurant's portions?
questions.explanation: The man says 'The portions are generous.'
questions.hint: A positive adjective describes the size of the food served.

options: Never — you always need a reservation
questions.explanation: The man says 'they accept walk-ins on weeknights.'
questions.hint: It depends on the day of the week.

questions.prompt: Why does the man offer to call ahead?
options: Because the restaurant can get busy
Because they want a private table
questions.explanation: He says 'it can get busy. I could call ahead just to be safe.'
questions.hint: He wants to avoid a problem on arrival.

questions.prompt: What time do they agree to go?
questions.explanation: The man says 'How about seven o'clock?'

questions.prompt: Is the restaurant smart or casual?
options: Casual — no need to dress up
questions.explanation: The man says 'It's a casual place, so you don't need to dress up.'
questions.hint: He tells her not to worry about dressing up.

questions.prompt: What does the woman decide to wear?
questions.explanation: She says 'I'll wear my blue dress then.'
questions.hint: She mentions a specific item of clothing and its colour.

questions.prompt: Why does the woman choose that outfit?
options: Because it is comfortable but looks nice
Because it matches the restaurant's style
questions.explanation: She says 'It's comfortable but still looks nice.'
questions.hint: She gives two reasons for her choice.

options: The man will make the booking
questions.explanation: The man says 'I'll make the booking now.'
questions.hint: One person takes responsibility for this task.

questions.prompt: What does the woman suggest if they enjoy the restaurant?
options: They should invite friends next time
They should make it their regular spot
They should try another Thai restaurant
questions.explanation: She says 'if we like it, we should make it our regular spot.'
questions.hint: She imagines going back again and again.

cloze.template: Man: Are you hungry? I thought we could go out for {{b1}} tonight. Woman: That sounds lovely! Do you have somewhere in {{b2}}? Man: There's a new Thai restaurant on George Street. But there's also that Italian place we {{b3}} to go to. Woman: I haven't tried Thai food in a long time. What's the Thai restaurant like? Man: A {{b4}} went last Friday and said the food was amazing. The portions are generous and it's not too expensive. Woman: Is it easy to get a table, or do we need a {{b5}}? Man: They accept {{b6}} on weeknights, but it can get busy. I could call ahead just to be safe. Woman: Yes, please do. What time were you thinking? Man: How about {{b7}} o'clock? That gives us plenty of time to get ready. Woman: Should I wear something smart, or is it {{b8}}? Man: It's a casual place, so you don't need to {{b9}}. Woman: I'll wear my blue {{b10}} then. It's comfortable but still looks nice. Man: Good idea. I'll make the {{b11}} now.

blanks.explanation: 'dinner' — the man suggests going out for dinner tonight.
'mind' — 'somewhere in mind' means he has a specific place he is thinking of.
'used' — 'used to go to' describes a past habit that no longer continues.
'colleague' — a work colleague visited the restaurant last Friday.
'reservation' — a reservation is an advance booking for a table at a restaurant.
'walk-ins' — walk-ins are customers who arrive without a prior reservation.
'casual' — the woman asks if the restaurant is casual or smart.
'dress up' — to dress up means to wear formal or smart clothes.
'dress' — she will wear her blue dress.
'booking' — 'make the booking' means to reserve a table.

criticalThinkingQuestion: The couple in this dialogue carefully considers factors like price, dress code, and whether a reservation is needed before choosing a restaurant. What factors do you think are most important when choosing where to eat, and how has technology changed the way people find and book restaurants?
